Nevertheless, we recommended and Management agreed to continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the MGC’s funds while limiting expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid cash deficiency.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE requires the MDRRMO to continuously monitor the implementation of the targeted PPAs funded by LDRRMF for the optimal utilization thereof in accordance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS and GFP coordinate closely with the Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned Department Heads in monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure the correctness of the reported GAD accomplishments and fund utilization.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Department Heads monitor the implementation of their targeted PPAs to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the allotted fund, if needed, to other prioritized PPAs to maximize the utilization thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Property Office maintains the RegSPI and other records and reports as required in the COA Circular 2022-004;
The MAO prepares and maintains the SPLC; and
The Inventory Committee conduct the annual physical inventory of semi-expendable properties, prepare and submit the RPCSP, and determine/provide the estimated useful life of each semi-expendable property.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the MA to:
Reconcile the difference in the CIB amounts in the FS and the corresponding SLs;
Present in the Notes to FS the amount of the CLT account, indicating the amounts of accountabilities per accountable officer; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of the MGC’s Cash Accounts with the MT, hence to decrease, if not to eliminate the noted discrepancies in the said accounts’ balances.

We also recommended and Management agreed to undertake the necessary actions to collect the unsettled accountabilities of the former MT.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE:
Call the attention of the Disposal and Appraisal Committee to handle the procedures in the disposal of unserviceable properties;
Instruct the Committee to dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the provisions of the Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties and other issuances related thereto; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to assist the Committee in preparing the IIRUP by providing necessary information, especially for those properties not yet included in the submitted IIRUP, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
MA to recognize the accrued interest payable and accrued interest expense in the financial statement as at year end;
MA to adjust the interest expense for December 18 to 31, 2024 recognized in CY 2025, to reflect the correct total interest expense; and
MA and the MBO to reconcile the discrepancy between the total loan payments and the amount obligated.
Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2024-006 on the recording of the issuance of the semi-expendable properties.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Utilize the allocated budget for the cost of processing and securing a Torrens Title for the untitled lots;
Secure copy of the Torrens Title of its lots drawn in the name of the Municipality from the Land Registration Authority and other concerned government agencies; and
Ensure proper safekeeping of the newly secured and subsequently acquired Land Titles.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Return to the concerned SA or the BTR, the unutilized balances of the fund transfers wherein the agreement requires the return thereof.  Otherwise, transfer as income to the GF if the agreement does not mention the disposition of the unused balance, subject to the approval of the grantor;
Monitor that future utilization thereof will be subject to the usual budgeting, accounting and auditing laws, rules and regulations; and
Make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balance of the Due to NGAs and other affected accounts.

We recommended that Management require the concerned implementing office/s of the MGC to step up their concerted efforts to ensure optimum utilization and implementation of the PPAs funded by NGAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desired benefits to its constituents.

We commended the Management for implementing the majority of its PPAs within the same budget year, thus substantially achieving the desired developmental impact in the community.  Nonetheless, we recommended and Management agreed to:
Continue to adopt strategies to achieve full implementation of the targeted PPAs and maximize utilization of the 20% DF for the substantial achievement of the purpose for which said fund was established/received; and
Consider the additional requirements in prioritizing the implementation of PPAs so that delays may be prevented and the same will be completed within the planned time frames.

Likewise, we recommended that the concerned departments reconcile their respective records/reports before submitting the same to the oversight agencies or the LCE for decision-making purpose.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to continue to remit all withheld taxes within the prescribed period as set by the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE:
Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking HDMF numbers of the job order personnel with unremitted contributions.  Otherwise, require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and inform immediately the MAO of the MID for remittance purposes; and
Continuously comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.

We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the AMT and the MA to submit on time the subject accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents. We also recommended that such activities be properly monitored so that reports are submitted promptly in accordance with Sections 100, 107 and 122 of PD No. 1445 and the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MA and the AMT submit immediately the subject DVs together with complete supporting documents and observe strictly the deadline set for the submission thereof as required under Sections 122 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006.

Likewise, we recommended that the MAO and property and supply unit continue the maintenance of updated PPE records and provide a computerized copy of all property/ledger cards for easy reference and maintenance of records.

Continue its improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing PPAs especially those funded under the continuing appropriations to maximize the utilization of said fund for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

observations.recommendation: Continue and enhance the PPAs that address issues related to SWM and environmental protection; Ensure that provisions of the contract entered by the MGC are followed; Instruct the MENRO to intensify the efforts to require the barangays for the utilization and full functionality of the barangay MRFs; and Instruct the MENRO together with other concerned departments to prepare an annual progress report on the strategies implemented, as well as an accomplishment as required in the NSWMC Resolution No. 1594, series of 2023.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented PPAs to address issues related to SWM & environmental protection such as Community clean up drive/campaign and Recycling Educational Program

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that all offices and personnel concerned continue to adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines with regards to PPE in order to safeguard the MGC’s largest asset classification.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and all other implementing offices of the MGC continue to maximize the utilization of the fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management instruct the MENRO to intensify efforts in requiring barangays to have their own MRF which should be fully utilized.

Also, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to submit promptly the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on or before the due dates in compliance with Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

Nevertheless, we recommended that the LCE instruct the implementing offices to facilitate full implementation of the remaining three on-going and unimplemented PPAs.  Also, require the MAO to return/refund the remaining balance of fully implemented PPAs to the SAs.

Nevertheless, we recommended that the LCE direct the MDRRM Council to:
monitor properly the utilization of LDDRMF;
include only eligible PPAs in the LDRRMFIP; and
ensure that charges against the LDRRMF were eligible expenses to ensure that government funds are properly utilized.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Municipal Engineer to provide the MA and the PO the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis for recording in the LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively;
PO to keep and maintain complete and updated LRNPCs using the prescribed format for checking the accuracy of the LRN account maintained by the MA; and
PO and the MA to conduct regular reconciliation of their records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other.

We recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the PO to initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties by preparing the IIRUP in accordance with Section 125 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I; and
After the disposal, require the PO to submit the necessary documents, together with the Official Receipt (OR), if any, to the MA, as basis in dropping the disposed unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance with the Section 448 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume II.

We recommended that the LCE require the Inventory Committee to identify properties without tags/stickers and attach the Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-examination with AREs, facilitate identification of the properties during inventory count, and to establish accountability and control in the custody or use of the properties.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The MT to deposit immediately and intact all undeposited collections to the AGDBs;
All concerned AOs to remit strictly to the MT all succeeding collections and the MT to deposit promptly and intact the collections; and
All AOs to adhere strictly to the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations to safeguard the cash from risk of loss, otherwise appertaining sanctions from the Revised Penal Code shall be applied against erring AOs.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA, through the MT to request on time from the AGDB the release of the monthly BS together with accompanying DMs and CMs and to follow-up incessantly such request until the BS are actually released by the bank.   Thereafter, direct the MA to record all book reconciling items identified in the BRS as required under COA Circular No. 96-011.
We likewise recommended that the MA prepare and submit the BRS to the AT within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the LCE require the MAO to compel the concerned AOs to settle immediately in full or refund their outstanding CAs.  Particularly, we recommended that the MAO issue demand letters to the 12 erring AOs to settle their long outstanding CAs.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the concerned offices to strictly enforce compliance with the required documentations prior to acceptance, processing and recording of transactions as valid for payment and to ensure that proper control procedures were undertaken and no regulations/provision of the law were overlooked;
Require from the person-in-charge/AO the immediate submission of the lacking documents or provide valid justifications for the inadequate documentation; and
Advise the concerned oversight offices to implement efficient internal control procedures to ensure that government funds are safeguarded and its intended purposes are served.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the MA to trace and analyze pertinent transactions and reconcile the discrepancy of ₱2.23 million in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.  Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the said accounts in the FS.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require:
The MA to exert extra effort in the retrieval of detailed information and documents on said receivables, if still possible;
The MT/MA to send demand letters to principal borrowers as well as to co-makers to intensify collection efforts.  Adopt legal remedies, if warranted, to effect collection from delinquent borrowers; and
The MA to analyze the collectability of the receivables.  Otherwise, request to the COA authority to write-off dormant accounts which could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the concerned offices to enforce strictly compliance with required documentations prior to acceptance, processing and recording of liquidation reports as valid transactions and to ensure that proper control procedures and rules and regulations are strictly observed;
Require from the barangays the immediate submission of lacking documents or provide valid justifications on the inadequacy of documentation; and
Advise the oversight offices to implement efficient internal control procedures to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from irregular disbursement.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management comply strictly with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the availability of the MOA as basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers.  Also, furnish the AT with the necessary MOA to verify whether the purposes of the trust funds are met.
We likewise recommended that the LCE require the MA to draw the necessary adjusting entry relative to the erroneous debits to Due to NGAs account of disbursements accruing to Trust Liability account.  Henceforth, record properly all transactions in the books to present accurate and reliable account balances.
We recommended that Management require the MT to provide the LSB with the correct income estimate of SET in the preparation of annual SEF Budget in compliance with the Local Government Code.
We recommended that the LCE direct the MHRM Officer to require the job order employees to apply for HDMF MID and inform immediately the MAO the availability thereof for remittance purposes.

We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned personnel to update their loan accounts and fully settle their overdue or matured loans with the GSIS to avoid imposition of penalties, surcharges and interest.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office, Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Team to step up their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties and its reconciliation with the books and records of the Accounting and Property Office, to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements (FS).

We further recommended that the concerned offices update the PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards, and the related Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment thereof and reconcile accordingly the contents and balances to facilitate the monitoring and verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office, Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Team to step up their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties and its reconciliation with the books and records of the Accounting and Property Office, to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the FS.
We further recommended that the concerned offices update the PPELCs, PCs, and the related AREs thereof and reconcile accordingly the contents and balances to facilitate the monitoring and verification.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant a Local Road Network Inventory with data as to components, fair value, condition, and the life expectancies of Infrastructure Assets particularly the Road Networks.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost and recognize component without corresponding cost at its fair value;
Prepare and maintain complete PPELC-LRNLC for each item of road network; and
Recompute the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense and thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balances of the Public Infrastructure and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require:
a.	All AOs to remit and deposit immediately all undeposited collections to the Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDBs) of the Municipality of Castilla; and
b. The collectors and Municipal Treasurer to deposit promptly and intact all succeeding collections of the LGU and to strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions to safeguard the cash from imminent danger.
Likewise, attention is invited to the provisions of the Revised Penal Code appertaining sanctions against erring AOs. More so, we recommended that the LCE require the Accounting and Treasurer’s offices to reconcile their records periodically to avoid accumulation of discrepancy in the Cash – Local Treasury account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect adjusting entries reverting the 43 stale checks equivalent to ₱324,764 to the Cash in Bank account and restore the corresponding payable accounts thereof. Henceforth, prepare and submit the BRS within the prescribed period so that outstanding and stale checks are monitored and appropriate actions are timely considered.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consistently monitor the release of checks issued by the Municipality and perform his task of informing the payees of the existence of checks due to them before said checks become stale. Likewise, adjust the cashbook records in the GF for the stale checks identified.

Management Comment:
Management assured that the observation will be looked into and immediate action will be facilitated as herein recommended.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations of proper recognition of inventories in the books of accounts;
Strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for the acquisition and issuance of supplies and materials;
Prepare and maintain SLCs for each commodity/stock; and
Record the issuance of supplies and materials to the related expense account/s on the basis of the SSMI.
We likewise reiterated that Management require the Property and Supply Officer to:
Prepare and maintain SCs for inventories which shall be regularly reconciled with the SLCs of the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit the same, together with the original copies of RIS, to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Present the current and non-current liability portions of the Loans Payable account and classify separately in the SFP to ensure fair presentation in the FS; and
Observe proper and complete disclosure requirements under IPSAS I in the preparation of the FS and Notes to FS for the ensuing year to provide sufficient and reliable information to its users.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the pertinent transactions and reconcile the variance of ₱2.81 million in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the said accounts.

We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in the retrieval of detailed information and documents on said receivables;
Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters and to intensify efforts to require the principal borrowers as well as the co-makers, if any, of those involved to pay the unpaid balances of their loans. In case of continuous default in payment of loans, seek assistance for the enforcement of legal remedies in the collection of long outstanding receivables and for the filing of appropriate charges without prejudice to the negligent officers and delinquent borrowers; and
Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the accounts to ascertain the collectability of the receivables and the probability of liquidation/refund from various accountable persons. Henceforth, consider requesting to the COA authority to write-off dormant accounts which could no longer be collected despite diligent efforts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require, thru the offices-in-charge to:
Review operations to determine the bottleneck and the reasons why the said grants were not yet implemented and utilized as to date and identify measures that would address the reasons and act accordingly;
Ensure timely and efficient utilization of funds more importantly those coming from the NGAs as required in the MOA, existing laws, rules and regulations; and
Make certain that the unused funds be promptly returned to the Source Agency.
We also recommended that Management designate a focal person who would be coordinating and responsible in monitoring the status of project and/or programs implementation of funds transferred from SAs, be it from NGA, GOCC or LGU.
We further recommended that Management require the concerned offices to charge and pay the bank charges from the GF in order not to have negative balances within the Trust Funds coming from the NGAs. Also, the accounting office personnel shall review thoroughly the entries upon recording to avoid errors.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect the proper adjusting entries to correct the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure the availability of the MOA in order to have a basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers and maintain timely FURs for monitoring purposes and submit to the Audit Team, for audit of the trust account balances.

We recommended that Management:
a. 	Require concerned implementing offices to reconcile their records with the records of SA as to the unliquidated, unrefunded and/or unremitted balances as to date;
b. 	Exert extra effort to secure the availability of significant documents relative thereto; and
c. 	Ensure prompt liquidation, remittance and/or refund of balances to the source or intended agency after the completion of each PPAs

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and identify the causes of the unreconciled amount between the concerned accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.
We further recommended that Management entail the Municipal Accountant to ensure that journal entry vouchers be simultaneously prepared as well as attach sufficient documentation upon receipt of funds on the part of the IA and upon issuance/transfer of funds on the part of the SA to avoid or at least minimize the risk of errors and be able to report fair, accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/index cards of all tax declarants within the Municipality and prepare accordingly the certified list of taxpayers, thereafter provide the Municipal Accountant a copy to be used as basis in the set-up and updating of RPT/SET receivable and deferred income accounts. Thus, updated records will be useful in intensifying collection strategies considering that individual payments could be well-monitored and defaulting taxpayers could be easily identified.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to consistently record and distribute discounts on advance payments as well as fines and penalties pertaining to RPT/SET in accordance with Sections 22 and 23 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs using the appropriate accounts described in COA Circular 2015-009.
We further reiterated that the Municipal Treasurer create and use a more comprehensive template to be used in the recording of collections wherein tax dues, discounts, and fines and penalties applicable for specific period could be identified to facilitate proper recording.

We recommended that the MPDO and all other implementing offices of the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.
We also recommended that Management strengthen the management/process of reporting information by improving its monitoring and evaluation capabilities and coordination among offices with the end view of producing more complete and accurate reports that are relevant in decision-making.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports as well as the year-end FS on or before the legal deadline in compliance with Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and her staff employ best efforts to uphold efficiency and effectiveness towards making quality and reliable financial reports.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the remaining unsubmitted DVs together with the complete relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of the same and other financial reports as required under Sections 122 and 127 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which are the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget to ensure an efficient and effective utilization of PPAs under the SEF and the attainment of its objectives, and to furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.
Moreover, we recommended that the SEF Budget be specifically presented by PPAs, by allotment class and per school. We also recommended that the Management take into consideration the appropriate charging from the budgeting up to record keeping to ascertain the reliability of information provided to users thereof.

We recommended that Management submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management direct the MDRRM Council to use the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to incorporate the PPAs to be funded as well as current appropriations pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to boost the attainment of a disaster resilient community.
Also, we recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP and the supplemental investment program, if any, within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:
Remit immediately the long-outstanding balances and ensure timely remittance of the monthly dues to avoid the imposition of penalties, surcharges and interest arising from delayed remittances;
Enjoin the concerned personnel to update their accounts and fully settle their overdue or matured loans with the GSIS; and
Exhaust all means to pay the obligations of their officials and employees who will be separated from the service, such as utilizing their Terminal Leave Benefits to avoid further accumulation of interests and penalties.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical inventory taking and submit to the COA Audit Team a complete and appropriate RPCPPE with balances duly reconciled with the accounting and other property records, after considering the necessary adjustments, if any.
We also recommended that the concerned officers update the PPELCs, PCs, and the related AREs thereof and accordingly reconcile their contents and balances to facilitate the monitoring and verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to trace the details of public infrastructures dropped from the books in CYs 2013 and 2014 and accordingly re-account the carrying values of such structures. If necessary, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to determine the status and the best estimates of the fair market values of such assets which could be used in the absence of historical data.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN; and
Direct the Inventory Committee and the GSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the formats prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the GSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects for monitoring purpose and to ensure proper recording/presentation and control of the assets.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to undertake the procedures necessary for the relocation/reidentification and proper disposal of all listed articles/properties in the IIRUP as at December 31, 2018 as well as those identified to be useless this period.
We likewise recommended that after disposal, the Municipal Accountant drop from the books the accounts and balances pertaining to the disposed items of PPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Treasurer to:
Immediately collect from the employees concerned the amounts due them and thereafter deposit said payments to the Municipality’s accounts in authorized government depository banks (AGDBs);
Deposit promptly and intact all succeeding collections of the LGU; and
Stop using the collections in accommodating personal cash needs of selected employees and in payments of illegitimate operating expenses.

We reiterated the previous audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive compel:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to identify the discrepancies in their records and accordingly reconcile the same. The report of such reconciliation must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor immediately;
The Municipal Treasurer to maintain comprehensive records of the cash balances of the LGU indicating therein the accountable officers liable for such balances; and
The concerned offices to henceforth comply with the requirements of Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I to produce accurate and reliable reports of the cash accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect adjusting entries reverting the 26 stale checks equivalent to ₱117,239.71 to the Cash in Bank account in the GF of the LGU and restore the corresponding payable accounts thereof. Henceforth, prepare and submit the BRS within the prescribed period so that outstanding and stale checks are monitored and appropriate actions are timely considered.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consistently monitor the release of checks issued by the Municipality and perform his task of informing the payees of the existence of checks due them before said checks become stale. Likewise, adjust the cashbook records in the GF for the stale checks identified.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of Castilla immediately pass a Resolution approving the closure of the existing account of the Municipality at the PNB and the immediate transfer of funds thereat to an AGDB. If determined to continue using PNB’s banking services, secure approval from the BLGF.

We recommended that Management compel the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/index cards of all tax declarants within the Municipality and accordingly prepare the certified list of taxpayers, thereafter provide the Accountant a copy to be used as basis in the set-up and updating of RPT/SET receivable and deferred income accounts. In this way, updated records will be useful in intensifying collection strategies considering that individual payments could be well-monitored and defaulting taxpayers could be easily identified.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake thorough review of the accounts affected including the posting errors identified herein and effect adjusting entries to reflect correct balances of the relevant accounts. Likewise, update the pertinent accounts upon receipt of the certified list of taxpayers.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to consistently account and apportion discounts on advance payments as well as fines and penalties pertaining to RPT/SET in accordance with Sections 22 and 23 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs using the appropriate accounts described in COA Circular 2015-009.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer create and use a more comprehensive template to be used in the recording of collections wherein tax dues, discounts, and fines and penalties applicable for specific period could be clearly identified.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to relevant laws and  rules on the submission of accounts and other required reports in order to allow timely evaluation, analysis and audit so that the concerned personnel could be notified immediately of the deficiencies/errors for better management of government resources.
We also recommended for the immediate submission of the 120 subject DVs to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials: 1. to submit purchase orders duly supported with all the required documents to the auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; 2. that the purchase orders be completely accomplished in all respects and see to it that not in the following required data/information invariably appear therein, viz: (a) Purchase Order Number (b) Date of Purchase Order (c) Complete name of supplier (d) Complete address of supplier (e) Information whether the supplier is a manufacturer or exclusive distributor; registered with SEC, DTI or both (f) Telephone and fax number of supplier, if any (g) Date of delivery (h) Complete item name (i) Complete detailed item specification, including accessories ie, narrative description, size/dimension/volume, model, use of item, color, capacity, horsepower, voltage, watts, gauge, kind of equipment where spare part is to be used whether replacement or original (for spare parts), part number of spare parts, type and classification of material, brand new or second hand, etc. (j) Price (individual unit price; if lot price any document showing the detailed breakdown of the cost should likewise be submitted) (k) Unit of measure when it is not universally accepted indicate numbers or weight, or lineal measure, i.e., if stated as per "roll", indicate the number of yards/meter per roll; if per "box", state number/pcs. contents; if per bag how many pounds or kilos (l) Quantity or number of units (m) Brand name. If no brand, state "none" (n) Country of manufacture or origin of item; i.e., local (or RP), or if foreign origin, specify country. (o) Terms; i.e., COD, 1/30 days, etc. (p) Mode of procurement; ie., public bidding, canvass, negotiated sale, and such other authorized modes. If "repeat order" indicate previous Purchase Order and date which was used as basis. (q) Taxes paid by agency; ie., VAT, etc. If taxes are paid by supplier, do not indicate.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
